Description: The Localizer is a utility class that stores localized bundles, resolves the active localization from the current language, listens to global application configuration changes, and emits an event when the language changes.

localizations.description: A record of language codes mapped to localization bundles. Each bundle may be present or undefined.

language.description: The active language used to resolve the current lexicon. The constructor initializes this value from the normalized browser language or from the normalized constructor argument. Later assignments through `language` or `setLanguage()` store the provided value as-is.
language.default: navigator.language normalized to base lowercase language code
resolutionNotes: Constructor normalization converts values such as `en-US` to `en`.
Subsequent assignments do not normalize and are stored verbatim.
Setting the same value or a falsy value does not emit an event.

lexicon.description: The resolved localization bundle for the current language.
lexicon.throws: Localizer: no localization bundles are available.

initialize.description: Registers the Localizer to listen for global application configuration changes on `window`.

dispose.description: Removes the global application configuration listener previously registered by `initialize()`.

setLanguage.description: Updates the active language by delegating to the `language` setter.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: The new language value to store. Falsy values are ignored.

onlanguagechange.description: Triggered when the active language changes to a new truthy value.
notTriggeredWhen: the assigned language equals the current language

onappconfigchange.description: A window-level custom event used to synchronize the Localizer with application configuration changes.
